notes: Shower oil category saturated on Amazon, 20K+ monthly sales for existing brands, no clear differentiation opportunity
O'Keeffe's Working Hands dominates this exact niche (10K+ monthly sales), extremely strong brand loyalty among trade workers
Nivea is already global brand available in US, blue tin is iconic and already sold everywhere
Weleda already operates in US market, well-established in natural/organic channel
Bamboo toothbrush market saturated, dozens of brands on Amazon, hard to differentiate on commodity product
Every beauty brand has micellar water, glass bottle/refill not enough differentiation
The Ordinary, Radha Beauty, and others saturate rosehip oil market at all price points
CBD sleep balms and lavender products extremely saturated, highly competitive category
Single-oil hair serums commodity product, available everywhere, no brand opportunity
Licorice flavor polarizing in US market, cultural preference doesn't translate
Handmade soap market extremely saturated, Etsy full of shea butter soaps, commodity product
Blue Lizard, Supergoop, Sun Bum own mineral stick sunscreen category, strong brand loyalty
Hemp beauty products saturated market post-CBD boom, hard to differentiate
Baby product used by adults is cultural quirk, hard to market in US without weird positioning
Tea tree cleansers everywhere (Body Shop, Desert Essence, etc.), drugstore product hard to premium-ize
Aztec Secret Clay dominates DIY mask powder category, strong cult following
